commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Headless, plugin-driven Rich Text Editor built on Lexical

commit.message:
feat(build): enable npm pinning enforcement in dependency scan (#838)

## Description

Enabled **npm pinning enforcement** in the CI dependency-pinning scan
workflow. Updated the workflow default to scan
`github-actions,npm,workflow-npm-commands`, activating automatic
detection of unpinned npm commands in GitHub Actions workflow `run:`
blocks.

Three PowerShell functions were added to *Test-DependencyPinning.ps1*:

- **`Test-NpmCommandLine`** — regex-based detection of unpinned npm
commands (`npm install`, `npm i`, `npm update`, `npm install-test`)
while excluding safe variants (`npm ci`, `npm run`, `npm test`, `npm
audit`)
- **`Get-WorkflowNpmCommandViolations`** — indentation-aware YAML parser
that tracks nesting depth to confine detection to actual shell content
and avoid flagging comments
- **`New-NpmCommandViolation`** — constructs violation objects with
Medium severity and remediation guidance

Pester test coverage was added with fixture files for both safe and
unsafe npm command patterns.

### Scope Change: npm Exact-Version Enforcement

This PR introduces a **strategic departure** from SHA-pinning for npm
dependencies. Unlike GitHub Actions (which use 40-character commit SHAs
for immutable references), npm dependencies use **exact-version
enforcement** (`X.Y.Z` with no `^`, `~`, `*`, or range operators).

**Rationale for exact-version enforcement over SHA-pinning:**

| Factor | GitHub Actions (SHA) | npm (Exact Version) |
|--------|---------------------|---------------------|
| Registry model | Git repositories with mutable tags | Immutable
registry artifacts |
| Integrity verification | SHA pins to specific commit | Lockfile
integrity hashes (SHA-512) |
| Audit compatibility | N/A | `npm audit` requires semver versions |
| Reproducibility | SHA guarantees exact code | Lockfile + exact version
guarantees exact code |
| Human readability | 40-char hex strings | Semantic version numbers |

The validation regex enforces exact versions: `^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$` — no
ranges, no prefixes, no wildcards.

### Follow-Up Hardening (commit `b8bd099`)

Additional improvements shipped on this branch:

- **pip ExcludePatterns** — Virtual environment directories (`.venv`,
`venv`, `.tox`, `.nox`, `__pypackages__`) are now excluded from pip
dependency scanning to avoid false positives
- **SARIF integration tests** — Expanded from 1 weak assertion to 8
focused tests validating SARIF 2.1.0 schema compliance, severity mapping
(High→error, Medium→warning), location structure, and metadata
properties
- **Dependency pinning documentation** — New
`docs/security/dependency-pinning.md` page documenting all pinning
strategies with rationale, validation rules, CI integration flowchart,
and a dedicated comparison of SHA-pinning vs exact-version enforcement
for npm

## Related Issue(s)

Closes #526

## Type of Change

Select all that apply:

**Code & Documentation:**

* [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change fixing an issue)
* [x] New feature (non-breaking change adding functionality)
* [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature causing existing functionality to
change)
* [x] Documentation update

**Infrastructure & Configuration:**

* [x] GitHub Actions workflow
* [ ] Linting configuration (markdown, PowerShell, etc.)
* [x] Security configuration
* [ ] DevContainer configuration
* [ ] Dependency update

**AI Artifacts:**

* [ ] Reviewed contribution with `prompt-builder` agent and addressed
all feedback
* [ ] Copilot instructions (`.github/instructions/*.instructions.md`)
* [ ] Copilot prompt (`.github/prompts/*.prompt.md`)
* [ ] Copilot agent (`.github/agents/*.agent.md`)
* [ ] Copilot skill (`.github/skills/*/SKILL.md`)

**Other:**

* [x] Script/automation (`.ps1`, `.sh`, `.py`)
* [ ] Other (please describe):

## Testing

Validated with 100 Pester tests covering:

- Detected 4 violations in multi-command fixture (install, update,
install-test, shorthand i)
- Returned zero violations for safe-only workflow (ci, run, test, audit)
- Handled nonexistent files gracefully
- Excluded commented-out commands
- Detected violations in multi-line run blocks with mixed safe/unsafe
commands
- SARIF 2.1.0 output: version, schema, tool driver, result count,
severity mapping (High→error, Medium→warning), location structure,
metadata properties (8 tests)
- pip ExcludePatterns for virtual environment directories

## Checklist

### Required Checks

* [x] Documentation is updated (if applicable)
* [x] Files follow existing naming conventions
* [x] Changes are backwards compatible (if applicable)
* [x] Tests added for new functionality (if applicable)

### Required Automated Checks

The following validation commands must pass before merging:

* [x] Markdown linting: `npm run lint:md`
* [x] Spell checking: `npm run spell-check`
* [x] Frontmatter validation: `npm run lint:frontmatter`
* [x] Skill structure validation: `npm run validate:skills`
* [x] Link validation: `npm run lint:md-links`
* [x] PowerShell analysis: `npm run lint:ps`
* [x] Plugin freshness: `npm run plugin:generate`

## Security Considerations

* [x] This PR does not contain any sensitive or NDA information
* [x] Any new dependencies have been reviewed for security issues
* [x] Security-related scripts follow the principle of least privilege

## Additional Notes

This PR enforces npm dependency pinning using exact-version enforcement
rather than SHA-pinning. The scanner uses regex-only analysis with no
code execution, and `Test-Path -LiteralPath` guards all file reads. Test
count increased from 93 to 100 with comprehensive SARIF integration
coverage.

---------

Signed-off-by: Marcel Bindseil <marcelbindseil@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Bill Berry <wbery@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Marcel Bindseil <marcelbindseil@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Jordan Knight <jakkaj@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Vaughan Knight <vaughan@vaughanknight.com>

commit.message:
Complete Phase 4: Auth/RBAC, CI/CD pipeline, database foundation

Authentication & RBAC:
- Keycloak JWT strategy with JWKS validation
- Hierarchical RBAC with closure table scope resolution
- PHI guard strips clinical data for non-medical roles
- Audit interceptor logs all data access
- Frontend auth with token management and protected routes

CI/CD Pipeline:
- ci.yml: lint → typecheck → test → build with Turbo caching
- security-scan.yml: npm audit + license check + SAST placeholder
- docker-build.yml: multi-stage Docker builds for web + API
- Dockerfiles: non-root, Iron Bank hardened, HEALTHCHECK enabled

Database Foundation:
- Drizzle migration runner and config
- Seed script: USAF/USSF hierarchy, roles, permissions, admin user
- Mock data generator: 500 personnel, 3500 readiness records
- Pre-generated sample-wing.json (6.4MB) for development

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 3 — boss mechanics, titles, tests, mobile polish (#19 #20 #21 #22)

- Fire breath warning on boss intro screen

Titles & Achievements (#20):
- Awards: Dragon Slayer (all enemies defeated), Perfect Scholar (no wrong answers),

- Title badges displayed on victory screen
- Titles persisted in questState.hero.titles

Unit Tests (#21):
- 16 new tests covering equipment, events, XP, leveling, boss, titles
- 114 total tests, all passing

Mobile UX Polish (#22):
- Battle strip shrinks to 100px on mobile (was 140px) to reduce scrolling
- Compact question clue padding, label spacing on mobile
- Hearts bar and XP bar margins reduced on mobile
- Battle canvas dynamically reads container height

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(parser): omit title key when undefined; add codeNode() helper

CodeNode.title is optional (title?: string). Previously all code-node
push sites used shorthand `{ ..., title }`, which always writes the key
even when title === undefined, making the object `{ title: undefined }`
rather than simply absent. deepStrictEqual() distinguishes these.

Add a codeNode(lang, text, title?) factory that only includes the title
key when the value is a string. Replace all six push sites with it.

Remove the now-redundant `title: undefined` from 44 expected objects in
parser.test.ts and renderer.test.ts (test refactor — the assertions still
verify the same behaviour, just without coupling to the undefined-key
implementation detail).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: wzshiming <6565744+wzshiming@users.noreply.github.com>

v1.4.4: simplify image capture to v1.3.4 Qt-only path

Remove over-engineered Win32 image fallback machinery:
  - Remove _try_capture_image, _retry_image_capture, _retry_pending
  - Remove _read_clipboard_format_bytes, _open_clipboard_with_retry
  - Remove _CF_PNG / _CF_JFIF module-level registration
  - Remove CF_BITMAP, CF_DIB, CF_DIBV5 constants
  - Remove kernel32 / ctypes restype fixups (no longer needed)
  - Remove 'import time'

Replace with the proven v1.3.4 inline approach in _process_clipboard:
  if mime.hasImage():
      img = clipboard.image()
  Uses bytesPerLine()*height() for safe QImage hash (not sizeInBytes).

All other new features retained: threading/_lock, suppress_next(),
set_exclusions(), _is_excluded_app(), _clipboard_has_exclude_flag()
(via IsClipboardFormatAvailable only), WM_WTSSESSION_CHANGE,
on_session_unlocked(), TYPE_EMAIL, TYPE_CODE, _looks_like_code(),
detect_sensitive(), _image_hash().

All 8 integration tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial release: APK Reverse Engineering Platform v0.1.0

Automated API discovery from Android apps combining static analysis
(jadx, string scanning, Dart binary extraction) with dynamic capture
(Frida SSL hooks, logcat Dio parsing, mitmproxy) to produce OpenAPI
3.0 specs, Postman collections, and curl command sheets.

Key features:
- Interactive capture mode with real-time endpoint discovery
- Auto-pull APK from device (no local file needed)
- Auto-detect ADB device
- Telemetry/analytics noise filtering
- Split APK handling for App Bundles
- JWT token extraction from heap dumps and SharedPreferences
- BoringSSL pattern scanning for Flutter release builds

Includes Bambu Handy analysis examples (50+ endpoints across 14
microservices discovered from live capture).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: apkre
repository.description: APK Reverse Engineering Platform — automated API discovery from Android apps. Extracts endpoints, auth tokens, schemas, and generates OpenAPI 3.0 specs via static analysis + dynamic capture (Frida, logcat, mitmproxy).

commit.message:
feat: Tool Loadouts / Toolbelts for Agent Tooling (#116)

* Initial plan

* Initial plan for Tool Loadout/Toolbelt feature

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement Tool Loadout/Toolbelt feature with tests

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add comprehensive Tool Loadout documentation

Co-authored-by: JerrettDavis <2610199+JerrettDavis@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add tool discovery system with auto-loadout scoping

Adds a dynamic tool discovery mechanism that allows agents to start with
a minimal toolset and discover/activate additional tools on demand. This
prevents context window overflow on small-context models (e.g. Foundry
Local 28K) while keeping all tools accessible.

New features:
- ToolDiscoveryTools: 4 kernel functions (discover_tools, activate_tool,
  list_tool_categories, list_toolbelts) for runtime tool management
- Auto-loadout selection: maps context window size to appropriate loadout
  (>=128K -> full, >=64K -> developer, <64K -> minimal)
- Loadout-aware scoping replaces crude "disable all tools" approach
- toolDiscovery plugin always preserved across all loadouts

Also fixes flaky SlidingWindowRateLimiter test with retry loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve formatting issues in SubagentRunner and ToolLoadoutTests

Remove alignment padding in switch expressions and fix import ordering
to satisfy dotnet format CI checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

perf(eval): skip test discovery on expression-only evals

Test discovery via LiveTestingHook.afterReload now only runs when:
- Methods were actually updated (function/type definitions), OR
- First eval needs initial project assembly scan

Expression-only evals (e.g. '1+1;;') skip discovery entirely,
eliminating the synchronous reflection overhead that caused slow
repeated evals.

Fixes #11

simplify starting the dev server from scratch (#5433)

* feat: add prestart/prestartSSL hooks to auto-build prerequisites

Automatically runs buildDLL:dev and buildModules before starting the
dev server if common-dist/common-manifest.json or package build outputs
are missing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move prestart build check into shared script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: check every buildable package for dist/index.js in prestart script

Previously used a glob that only checked if any dist/index.js existed,
not all of them. Now iterates packages with a build script to ensure
every one has its artifact before skipping buildModules.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use next available port if 3000 is already in use

Uses webpack-dev-server's built-in 'auto' port selection so yarn start
doesn't fail with EADDRINUSE when port 3000 is taken. Can still be
overridden with PORT=<n> yarn start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert: remove auto port selection change

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add set -euo pipefail to prestart script for fail-fast behavior

Ensures a failed buildDLL:dev or buildModules stops the process
immediately rather than continuing and hitting the same missing
artifact error downstream.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update pos-customer/openapi.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add progress indicator and stop button for in-flight requests (#155)

* feat: add progress indicator and stop button for in-flight requests

Add processing phase state machine (idle → thinking → generating) to
replace binary _isProcessing flag. Show animated "thinking..." indicator
during request setup and "generating..." during streaming. Add stop
button that cancels in-flight requests via session.abort().

Closes #122

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add processing phase and stop button tests

Add 7 tests covering the processing phase state machine:
- thinking/generating/idle phase transitions
- idle-on-error recovery
- full lifecycle sequence
- stopRequest triggering session.abort()
- stopRequest no-op when idle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* squad: progress indicator + stop button orchestration logs, session summary, and decision merge

Scribe merge:
- Orchestration logs for MacReady (architecture), Blair (implementation), Windows (testing)
- Session log for progress indicator + stop button work
- Merged 3 decision inbox files into decisions.md (no duplicates; consolidated into single entry)
- Deleted inbox files after merge

Issue resolved: #122 (Stop Button Robustness)

Work summary:
- 4-state processing phase state machine (idle → thinking → generating → idle)
- Progress indicator (animated dots) with dynamic text
- Stop button with session.abort() cancellation
- 7 new tests, all 301 tests passing
- Zero regressions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: swap context bar layout and merge send/stop button

Move attach buttons (Selection, File) to left side with workspace
indicator on the right. Replace separate stop button with send/stop
toggle — Send button becomes Stop during processing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review — stop/cancel lifecycle and race conditions

- Add request-scoped cancellation flag for thinking phase stop
- Track request ID to prevent finally-block clobbering
- Properly await session.abort() with error logging
- Add safety timeout for stuck stop button state
- Block duplicate sends during thinking phase

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add cancel lifecycle and race condition tests

Tests for thinking-phase cancellation, request ID clobbering
prevention, abort error handling, and duplicate send prevention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update windows history with cancel lifecycle testing learnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: robpitcher <robpitcher@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update pos-customer/openapi.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add invisible pane borders after colorscheme load

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(daemon): filter resumed sessions by working directory

Only resume sessions whose WorkingDir is under the daemon's current
directory (or vice versa). Sessions from unrelated directories are
logged and skipped instead of loading unconditionally.

Closes #28, Closes #27

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  - #458 (P2): Resume session missing additionalArgs

  - Branch strategy: hotfix from release/v0.1.x (not cherry-pick from master)
  - Routing: #456 to copilot, #457-#458 to Morty (flagged for review)

Closes #394

* fix: address PR #448 review — add BaseTreeProvider tests, fix hardcoded strings

Review fixes for squad/394-base-tree-provider (PR #448):

1. Add comprehensive base-tree-provider.test.ts (68 tests) covering:
   - _cleanNodeId, level filter CRUD, _contextWithFilter
   - matchesLabelFilter (OR within group, AND across groups)
   - _getFilterDescription, fetchAll debouncing
   - ADO/GitHub node builders, getChildren routing
   - _getAvailableOptionsBase, runtime filter integration

2. Replace hardcoded string literals in subclasses with inherited
   base class properties (_ghIdPrefix, _adoIdPrefix):
   - work-items-tree.ts: 4 occurrences (github-repo, github:, ado-project)
   - prs-tree.ts: 4 occurrences (github-pr-repo, github-pr:, ado-pr-project)

3. Confirmed: editless-tree.ts does NOT extend BaseTreeProvider
   (PR description mismatch noted — only prs-tree.ts and
   work-items-tree.ts are subclasses).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
